U.S. Signals Deripaska Should Cut EN+ Stake to 40% Range, Source Says

The U.S. Treasury Department has indicated it may allow Russian billionaire Oleg Deripaska to keep his ownership of En+ Group PLC in the range of 40 percent under a deal that would spare the company from sanctions, a person familiar with the matter said.

While Treasury hasn’t provided a specific target for Deripaska’s holding it had generally signaled his stake would need to be meaningfully below 50 percent, said the person, who spoke on condition of anonymity. Deripaska currently owns about 66 percent of the company.

A Treasury Department spokesman declined to comment, citing a policy against speaking publicly on pending petitions for sanctions relief. A spokesman for Deripaska also declined to comment.


Deripaska is among a group of Russian tycoons and companies with ties to Russian President Vladimir Putin who were hit with U.S. sanctions April 6 in retribution for the Kremlin’s alleged meddling in the 2016 presidential election. The sanctions initially disrupted the global supply chain for aluminum and sent prices soaring because the targets included United Co. Rusal, Russia’s largest producer of the metal.

En+ announced that Deripaska was stepping down from the boards of En+ and Rusal. En+ is expected to present a new board to Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control by the end of June, and apply to be removed from the sanctions list in July, the person said. The company’s lawyers in Washington are engaged daily with Treasury’s sanctions office.

En+, which owns about 48 percent of Rusal, has applied for an extension to avoid being delisted by the London Stock Exchange in order to cut Deripaska’s stake and appoint a majority of independent directors. Barring an extension, the LSE said En+ would be delisted June 1.

The U.S. has been receptive to working with En+ and Rusal -- Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin has said he isn’t seeking to put the Russian aluminum giant out of business. Mnuchin in late April extended a deadline for companies to wind down dealings with Rusal by almost five months to October.

By stating that Deripaska must cut his stake below 50 percent, Mnuchin offered an unusually explicit formula for a sanctioned entity to avoid U.S. sanctions. The move is an indication that the Trump administration wants to target Putin’s allies without causing broader economic harm.

https://www.cnbc.com/2018/05/31/reuters-america-exclusive-russias-deripaska-empire-bets-on-mid-summer-plan-to-escape-u-s-sanctions.html

美國商務部長羅斯（Wilbur Ross）周四宣佈，6月1日起向歐盟、加拿大及墨西哥分別徵收25%鋼鐵及10%鋁材關稅。另外他擬於周五訪華。

歐盟委員會主席容克坦言，「完全不能接受」美國徵收關稅，揚言會盡快公佈對策。

另外較早前獲得暫時豁免的澳洲、阿根廷及巴西則同意將入口美國的金屬「數量減少」，以換取永久性豁免。

美國總統特朗普今年3月宣佈向鋼鐵及鋁材分別徵收25%及10%進口關稅，但對歐盟、澳洲及加拿大等盟友提供豁免至本周五。較早前有傳媒報道，美國有意提早向歐洲鋼、鋁徵收關稅，另有報道指美國總統特朗普有意令德國汽車「退出」美國市場。

replies.msg: 出口量比上月上升番
同入手價比跌左好多

Interfax news agency reported that Russian aluminium producer Rusal , placed under US sanctions in April, increased aluminium exports by 49 % in June. Rusal, the world’s largest aluminium producer outside China, saw its exports and internal supply chain crippled after Washington put the company and its co-owner Oleg Deripaska on a blacklist in response to Russia’s alleged meddling in US elections.

Deripaska and his team are working to persuade the US Treasury Department to lift the sanctions, which stipulate that US customers must wind down business with Rusal by Oct 23.

In the meantime, aluminium shipments continue, with Rusal smelters shipping 294,000 tonnes abroad in June, Interfax said, citing data from Russian Railways.

The company’s exports of aluminium in the period between January and June this year, at 1.27 million tonnes, were 9 percent lower than during the same period the previous year, Interfax said, citing the data.

Last year, Rusal, in which Deripaska’s En+ is a controlling shareholder, sold a total of 3.95 million tonnes of aluminium, of which 42 % was shipped to Europe.
Source : REUTERS

Deripaska agreed in April to cut his stake in En+ to less than 50 percent from 66 percent currently and to appoint a majority of independent directors to the company’s board as part of a plan to persuade the U.S. to lift sanctions. 金手指：政變、貿戰、俄鋁 - 孫柏文
1小時前712
投資

特朗普喺未來兩個月，都會超被動，因為被11月初國會中期選舉牽制。　資料圖片

今周開市，恒指繼續跌。睇華文傳媒，就話因特朗普要再搞大對大陸嘅關稅。講真，真正理由係上周美帝出咗就業數據，人工升幅好大，令花旗國股市插鑊甘。
之前啲人仲估今次加息浪只會去到2019年（甚至今年年尾已加完）。不過出完數據，就開始諗息口會否真係不斷加到2020，甚至重返5厘、6厘。真係唔敢諗到時港樓價會點。
至於貿戰，華文傳媒仲喺度估特朗普想點。其實，特朗普喺未來兩個月，都會超被動，因為被11月初本土中期選舉牽制。花旗民主黨初選，啲老人家一個又一個被少壯超左膠射下馬。留得低嘅，就要左膠上身。左膠一貫係自己鍾意嘅嘢要免費（用「呢個係基本人權」做包裝），唔鍾意嘅嘢就要政府禁。佢哋唔鍾意特朗普，所以就算民選都好，要禁，即係政變。民主黨贏，就會隊佢落台。
其實呢場政變，成功與否，先係左右貿戰關鍵。特朗普輸，就冇得收番。佢贏，就可以再講數。所以中央已擺出態度，等中期選舉之後再算。
至於俄鋁（486），佢哋賣嘅鋁種類，溢價越升越有，因啲人真係驚10月尾制裁會落實。10月尾，即中期選舉前。如要我估，唔會有定案住，我諗又會延期。不過大家買咗嘅，要有心理準備冇延期，要將股票提倉，兼停牌。我們已成沙粒，飛沙風中轉。

孫柏文
競價

外電引述知情人士透露，俄羅斯鋁業巨頭俄鋁(00486)因受美國制裁而無法開展年度合同談判，為此正準備減產。

知情人士稱，作為中國以外最大的鋁供應商，俄鋁可能於本月開始限制包括西伯利亞地區的產量。俄鋁業務構成全球供應鏈的關鍵一環，故鋁行業可能會陷入又一場危機。

美國允許已簽署供應協議的俄鋁客戶繼續與該公司做生意，直到10月23日為止，但不得簽署新的合同。
